# Questions in Livestream 127

Live Stream #127: Pyramid of the Puppet Pharaohs

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=354" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">5:54</a> | Is there an Egyptian version of Lilith? | Sort of, but he explains Lilith mainly as a later Second Temple and Talmudic figure rather than a direct Egyptian equivalent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=646" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:46</a> | Did Moses have horns, or does Exodus 34:33-35 mean his face shone? | He reads the passage as Moses' face shining, not as Moses literally having horns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=973" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:13</a> | Does the etymology of names in Genesis tell us anything about the time or people Genesis refers to? | Not much. He says many of the names are too vague or uncertain to carry that kind of historical weight. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=1178" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:38</a> | Would the biblical authors have embraced the idea of God's absolute immutability as defended by Aristotle and Aquinas? | He says yes, and points to biblical language about God's unchangeability. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=1492" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">24:52</a> | Do Talmudic claims about Bezalel's miraculous craftsmanship fit the Exodus text? | He reads the text itself as emphasizing Spirit-given wisdom and skill without needing every later embellishment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=2143" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:43</a> | What are his thoughts on the work of John Hilber and Victor Matthews? | He cannot say much about Matthews, but he generally likes Hilber's Old Testament work when Hilber stays in that lane. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=2500" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:40</a> | Why are Christians not observing Torah now when the commands say "forever" in many places? | He explains the issue through fulfillment in Christ, covenant change, and the distinction between moral and ritual law. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=4462" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:22</a> | Can Shasu and Apiru be considered synonyms? | No. He distinguishes Shasu as wandering tribal groups and Apiru as a different social category. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=4727" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:47</a> | Did ancient Egypt have smoke-fumigation rituals for healing and cleanliness? | Yes. He identifies the practice as burning incense or fragrant wood for purifying and ritual purposes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=4924" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:04</a> | What is his favorite research library that he has visited? | He names the University of Liverpool's library because of the resources he used there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=5131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:25:31</a> | Does he think John son of Zebedee wrote the Gospel of John, or someone else? | He says he is not certain and keeps the answer cautious. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=5248" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:28</a> | How did Egyptians practice prayer, and did they have anything like mystical or ecstatic prayer? | He says Egyptians did practice prayer, and then compares Egyptian and Israelite prayer with caveats. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=5491" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:31</a> | What is the history of the Exodus mythicist position, and who knows more Latin? | He says Exodus mythicism grows out of critical and minimalist scholarship, and jokes that Dark Blade probably knows more Latin. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=5668" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:28</a> | Do Canadians learn other languages besides French? | French is required in school, though how much one learns depends on the student and program. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=8286" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:06</a> | If he could hang out with John Wesley, John Calvin, or Charles Spurgeon, who would it be? | He chooses from the options conversationally, treating it as a light historical-personality question. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=8729" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:29</a> | What are his thoughts on the Rashi and Walton views of Genesis 3:15? | He says he usually agrees with Walton but would need to read the specific claim; he treats Genesis 3:15 as having competing interpretive traditions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=9324" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:24</a> | What are his thoughts on alleged incidents of some Jews in the Holy Land spitting on Christian visitors? | He says people should not treat each other that way and frames it as bad behavior rather than something to excuse. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=9663" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:41:03</a> | Were badly handicapped Egyptian babies thrown into the Nile? | No. He says Egyptians generally abhorred infanticide. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=9754" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:34</a> | Which pharaoh does he think was involved in the killing of Israelite boys? | He says it was probably King Aye, while acknowledging the reconstruction is contextual. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=9861" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:44:21</a> | Is it true that some Jews at Elephantine worshiped Anat? | Yes. He says they worshiped Anat and other deities they should not have worshiped. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=10317" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:51:57</a> | Would it be legal to offer animals publicly for a rebuilt temple? | He says animal-rights activists would object, but frames the issue as one of public sanction and ritual context. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=10494" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:54</a> | Why does he think some will try to rebuild the temple? | He connects the expectation to New Testament language about the abomination of desolation in a holy place. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=10776" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:36</a> | Do we know what hieroglyphs Joseph's Egyptian name would have used? | He says we have a good idea and begins working from the Egyptian form of the name. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=10955" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:02:35</a> | If Egyptians found infanticide abominable, did that include abortion and abortion facilities? | Yes for abortion as abominable; he says Egypt did not have abortion facilities like those known elsewhere. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=11000" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:20</a> | What happened if an arranged marriage in Egypt failed because one person disliked the other? | He says divorce was available and nobody was compelled to remain in the marriage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=11860" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:17:40</a> | Why did Egypt not send wives to foreign courts? | He says Egypt viewed the other countries as beneath them and had neither the need nor desire to send royal women. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=12043" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:43</a> | How were the descendants of foreign wives seen if they were not murdered, and how did they fit into society? | They were worked into the bureaucracy, army, or court service rather than put in line for the throne. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=12196" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:23:16</a> | Did Israelite kings copy Egyptians in taking multiple wives? | He says they copied a broad ancient Near Eastern royal custom, not only Egypt. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=12290" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:24:50</a> | What about royal multiple-wife practices in the ancient Far East? | He says Chinese emperors had harems, while he is less certain about Korea and Japan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=12440" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:27:20</a> | Was removing shoes before a god an Egyptian practice, and is it evidence for the Torah? | No. He says Egyptians replaced shoes after ritual bathing rather than removing them before a god. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/hqbQIlTCGTE?t=12548" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:29:08</a> | Is Hur the father of Uri the same Hur who helped hold Moses' arms up? | Yes. He identifies Hur as the same person and notes the Egyptian connection of the name. |
